titlestmt.titleproper: Pierre Devinoy Collection of Medieval, Renaissance and Baroque Architecture Photographs
Pierre Devinoy Collection of Medieval, Renaissance and Baroque Architecture Photographs
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Robert Carlucci

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid prepared according to <title xlink:type="simple" render="italic">Describing Archives: A Content Standard (DACS)</title>.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday October 5, 2018 at 23:12
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.

did.unittitle: Pierre Devinoy Collection of Medieval, Renaissance and Baroque Architecture Photographs
did.abstract: Miscellaneous collection of black and white unmounted photographs and glass negatives of French medieval architecture and sculpture and Italian Renaissance and Baroque architecture by the photographer Pierre Devinoy. Glass negatives for much of the printed photograph material, with numbered inventory in Box 1. Most photographs with limited identification in pencil. Made by Pierre Devinoy, donated to the Visual Resources Collection by Sumner Crosby in 1969.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has not been transferred to Yale University. Further use of this material may be subject to the copyright laws of the United States (Title 17, United States Code) or to site license or other rights management terms or conditions.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Pierre Devinoy Collection of Medieval, Renaissance and Baroque Architecture Photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Miscellaneous collection of black and white unmounted photographs and glass negatives of French medieval architecture and sculpture and Italian Renaissance and Baroque architecture by the photographer Pierre Devinoy. Glass negatives for much of the printed photograph material, with numbered inventory in Box 1. Most photographs with limited identification in pencil. Made by Pierre Devinoy, donated to the Visual Resources Collection by Sumner Crosby in 1969.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Photographs in folders, arranged alphabetically by city and, usually, name of building. One folder with partial lists. Negatives in boxed sets or loose in archival containers.

did.unittitle: Numbered Inventories of glass negatives related to this photograph collection.
Cosne, Portal of S. Aignar (2 photographs)
Le Puy, St. Michel d'Aiguilhe (5 photographs)
Nimes, Cathedral of Notre-Dame and St. Castor (4 photographs)
Saint-Remy-de-Provence, Cloister of St. Paul du Mausolée (7 photographs)
Saint-Remy de Provence, Mausoleum, Triumphal Arch (9 photographs)
Renaissance and Baroque Architecture in Italy photographs, Miscellaneous Places A-L, including:
Renaissance and Baroque Architecture in Italy photographs, Miscellaneous Places M-Z, including:
Glass negatives, 4 x 6 in., of items from the print photographs in boxes 1-4 of this collection, including negatives numbered 602- 690.
Glass negatives, 4 x 6 in., of items from the print photographs in boxes 1-4 of this collection, including negatives numbered 691-751, plus a collection of acetate negatives for Amiens Cathedral.
Acetate negatives for the Italian Renaissance and Baroque photographs in Boxes 3 and 4 of this collection.
Glass negatives of French Gothic Cathedrals, including
Glass negatives of French Gothic Cathedrals, including:
